title: Flyway Migration Best Practices
question: You need to add a 'completed_at' timestamp column to the tasks table. The V3__create_tasks.sql migration has already been applied. What should you do?
options: Edit V3__create_tasks.sql to add the column
Delete the flyway_schema_history table and re-run all migrations
Add the column directly to the entity and let Hibernate update the schema
explanation: Flyway migrations are immutable once applied. You should never edit an already-applied migration, as Flyway checksums will detect the change and fail. Instead, create a new migration file (V4__add_completed_at_to_tasks.sql) with an ALTER TABLE statement to add the column. Deleting flyway_schema_history or relying on Hibernate auto-DDL are unsafe practices.
